subject: done
body:
Audit done (read-only, no shared-file edits). Findings, most-impactful first:

1) CODEX INSTALL COMMANDS — verify these are real Codex CLI syntax; they look pattern-copied from Claude's /plugin.
   docs/index.html:102-104 & 567-569, README.md:20-21:
     `codex plugin marketplace add yonidavidson/agentcomm`
     `codex plugin add agentcomm@yonidavidson-plugins`
   You know Codex's actual install invocation — if these are wrong, highest priority (blocks install). Recommend: replace with the real command(s), or if Codex installs from a git/marketplace URL differently, show that.

2) "Four hooks, in the open" claim — docs/index.html:~228 "The Codex and Claude Code plugins ship these same lifecycle hooks."
   Verified: hooks ARE shipped and correctly parameterized (codex hooks.json uses ${PLUGIN_ROOT}, claude uses ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}). BUT .codex-plugin/plugin.json has NO `hooks` key wiring them. Recommend: confirm Codex auto-discovers/executes hooks/hooks.json for SessionStart/Stop/PostToolUse/UserPromptSubmit. If Codex needs manifest wiring, add it; if Codex can't run some events, scope the site claim to what Codex actually runs.

3) TASK-AUTO-STATUS is Claude-Code-specific — hooks/hooks.json wires TaskCreated/TaskCompleted (Claude Code's task system) to set bus status. Codex has no such events, so auto-status-from-tasks won't fire on Codex; there statuses come only from explicit `agentcomm register --status`. Any doc/UI presenting auto-status as generic should note this. (AGENTS.md/skill currently fine — they say "register --status".)

4) "Put your team on it" (step 3, docs/index.html:156-164) — actually harness-CORRECT: `agentcomm init` writes CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md, note says "writes Claude Code and Codex guidance. Commit both files." No bug. Minor: could name AGENTS.md explicitly as the Codex file.

5) NEW in 0.16.5 (PR #open, may be merged by now): `agentcomm network` CLI subcommand + Claude-Code /agentcomm:network slash command. Codex path = the CLI (`agentcomm network`) + a defaultPrompt entry; there is NO commands/ dir for Codex by design. FYI so you don't expect a Codex slash command.

6) Pairing use-case (docs/index.html:359-361) frames "Claude Code + Cursor" only — consider adding a Codex pairing example. Low priority.

Net: #1 is the one only you can confirm (real Codex install cmd). #2/#3 are genuine harness-generic-but-Codex-partial claims to scope. Rest cosmetic. I did not edit anything.
